methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Late Stage → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $5.7B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 2 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
The Boring Company is a late-stage transportation infrastructure venture that has raised $675M to date and carries a current post-money valuation of $5.7B. The peer universe in transportation is limited; four private comparables exist (Rivian, Einride, Harbinger, and REGENT Craft), though none qualify as sufficiently tight matches on stage and valuation tier to support a robust peer-median method. No public transportation infrastructure companies provide direct comparability.

Given the constrained peer set, stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable triangulation approach. Applying a 1.2x step-up multiplier—standard for late-stage advancement—to the $5.7B last post-money valuation yields $6.8B. This reflects expected value accretion as the company progresses toward Series or subsequent funding rounds. The peer-median approach was not applied due to insufficient narrow-stage comparables; broadening to all transportation peers would introduce undue noise.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $5.8B to $7.9B, with a median of $6.8B. The current $5.7B valuation sits near the low end of this range, implying modest upside under standard late-stage progression assumptions. Key uncertainties include the absence of tight peer benchmarks, execution risk specific to transportation infrastructure deployment, and limited public market precedent for this subsector. Confidence in this range is moderate, anchored to a single method with a 1.2x multiplier that may not fully capture company-specific risk or market conditions.
